tldr: Nothing shipped this week — git log since 2026-05-22 shows only last Saturday's audit commit (449056f). The backlog from last run carries over intact (items #12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #25, #32) and two new items are added this run (#33, #34) from this week's industry signals.

Search visibility: 41 queries re-checked against live SERPs on 2026-05-29. **MERIDIAN'S BIGGEST RANK MOVE TO DATE — meridianosteopathy.co.nz climbed from non-aggregator position 5 → 3 for 'osteopath vs physiotherapist christchurch', overtaking christchurchosteopath.co.nz (Moorhouse Osteopathic Centre) which slipped to #5.** Second consecutive run we've surfaced in any tracked top-10, and the first time we've out-ranked an NZ-side competitor on a tracked query. Halswell Clinic's blog post still holds #1 — they have a dedicated osteopath-vs-physio-vs-chiro URL, we don't, and we're climbing without one (homepage broadened-title from PR #78 + citation-ready intros from PR #77 + apex consolidation from PR #73 still doing the work). Priority bumped to 35 per the ≤3 rank rule. Every other tracked query stayed absent from top 10, so the standard -10 decay applies to the remaining 37 + 3 first-time checks. Notable observations: 'osteopath hillmorton' is STILL all UK results (sanderstead-osteopaths, fionarobertson.net, longmoreosteopaths.com) 19 days after item #29 (Hillmorton in flowing intro copy) — persistent indexing lag on suburb-tail queries continues; 'osteopath wigram' and 'osteopath addington' also remain NZ-side hollow. All three queries added last run (post-concussion, lower-back-pain, tennis-elbow) got first-time checks and none ranked — Better Health holds two URL variants on tennis-elbow (`/resources/...` + `/conditions/elbow-pain/`), the 'multiple URL variants per condition' pattern is the structural lesson of this audit. Three new derived queries added to the pool ('rotator cuff osteopath christchurch', 'postnatal osteopath christchurch', 'chiropractor vs osteopath christchurch') — last one tracks whether our comparison-intent homepage surfacing extends to osteopath-vs-chiropractor too.

Industry signals this week: (1) Ahrefs (week of 2026-05-26) — only **38% of AI Overview citations now come from top-10 organic results, down from 76% in Jul-2025**, on the back of Gemini 3's query fan-out, which validates the 'stay citation-ready even when you can't out-rank Better Health' thesis from #31/#77; (2) Search Engine Land (week of 2026-05-26) — 25,000-URL study shows AI search disproportionately cites list-formatted pages with structured H2/H3s and ~18-word items (drives new item #34); (3) Cloudflare (week of 2026-05-26) — **Claude-SearchBot is now a distinct user-agent at ~2% crawler share, separate from the training-only ClaudeBot we already allow** (drives new item #33); (4) Search Engine Journal (week of 2026-05-26) — Google officially deprioritises llms.txt for AI Overviews / AI Mode (Lighthouse audit only, not ranking signal) — no rollback needed since we already ship it cheaply, but no extra weight in roadmap priority; (5) mapranks / icreatebrand (2026-05-25) — Google is replacing Maps Q&A with Gemini-generated answers pulled from GBP + reviews + the clinic website, so PR #77's citation-ready first paragraphs are doing the right work here too. Backlog after this run: 9 items pending (#12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #25, #32, #33, #34). Tier 1 → #33 (robots.txt Claude-SearchBot allow). Tier 2 → #12, #13, #25, #32, #34. Tier 3 → #14, #15, #16.
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images
Site-wide sweep removing 'GP referral' from patient-facing copy (per the CLAUDE.md copy rule); condition CTA banners simplified to heading + booking button only

Google Business Profile services audit completed manually (item #21) — owner added Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine to GBP service list

/conditions/neck-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, mirroring the four item-#10 pages; covers acute vs chronic, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links back to the osteopathy service (item #22, PR #63)

/conditions/back-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema; covers acute vs chronic, sciatica overlap, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links to /conditions/sciatica/ and the osteopathy service (item #23, PR #64)

<link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown" href="/llms.txt"> + the same for /llms-full.txt added in the HTML head — signals AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists alongside the HTML (item #26, PR #62)

Physician schema upgraded to Schema.org v30 IndividualPhysician on every team profile, with practicesAt: { @id: '#clinic' } linking each practitioner to the MedicalClinic node — sharpens entity disambiguation for ChatGPT / Perplexity / AI Overviews (item #27, PR #65)

Image alt text rewritten descriptive-not-titular across team profiles (team-profile.njk now uses member.photoAlt with name fallback), condition pages, and service pages — each now describes what's visible plus relevant clinical context. Closes G14 (item #28, PR #70 / bundled in #71)

Hillmorton + Wigram + Addington + Halswell + Cashmere now appear in flowing intro copy on every service page in services.json (osteopathy, acupuncture, herbal medicine), not just the site-wide footer line. Closes G22 (item #29, PR #68 / bundled in #71)

'Conditions we commonly treat' block added to the osteopathy service page linking all six condition pages (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery), plus a matching 'Common conditions we treat' section on the homepage with high-intent button links — gives the condition pages an internal-link surface beyond the nav dropdown so Google can pass relevance signals to them. Closes G23 (item #30, PR #69 / bundled in #71)

/book/ page made AI-citable — added ReserveAction schema and visible intro content so ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini can recommend it as a booking action, not just an interstitial. Especially relevant after Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with EHR integration, since AI assistants are increasingly handling the appointment-booking step (PR #72)

Explicit www → apex 301 redirects added in netlify.toml — Google now consolidates link equity onto the canonical meridianosteopathy.co.nz apex domain rather than splitting it between www and apex variants (PR #73)

/services/medical-acupuncture/ renamed to 'Acupuncture & Dry Needling' across nav, homepage card, JSON-LD, breadcrumbs and inter-service cards; Min Jin positioned as TCM lead and Nina Hu as Western medical acupuncture / dry needling lead; new FAQ entries on acupuncture-vs-dry-needling, which practitioner to book, and what to expect at a first dry-needling session. Implementation diverged from the original spec (which had been a separate /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page) — the rename achieves the same G16 goal of one indexable URL targeting Western dry-needling intent, without splitting authority across two pages. Closes G16 (item #17, PR #78)

Every condition page (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery) and every service page (osteopathy, acupuncture, herbal-medicine) now opens with a 40–60 word citation-ready direct answer in the 'X treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch …' format, followed by the existing evidence-informed prose as the second paragraph onward. The answer-leading sentence also becomes the first sentence of the MedicalCondition / Service JSON-LD description field, so it propagates to AI-citation crawlers. Closes G24 (item #31, PR #77)

Per-practitioner booking pages added — each practitioner now has their own /book/<slug>/ URL with Cliniko pre-targeted to their calendar (no more landing on the generic 'select a practitioner' page after a homepage card click); homepage practitioner cards repointed at the matching per-practitioner /book/ page. Reduces clicks from 'pick a practitioner' to 'pick a time' from 3 to 1 (PRs #80, #81)

Min Jin's ACC eligibility correctly distinguished from Nina Hu's across acupuncture pages and per-practitioner booking pages — Nina is an ACC-registered osteopath who lodges claims in clinic and integrates dry needling within ACC-subsidised consultations; Min is registered to treat as an ACC acupuncture provider but does not lodge new claims herself. Wording on /book/min-jin/ adjusted accordingly (PR #82)
Kaylee Frost team photo updated, colour-corrected, and optimised across multiple iterations (PRs #86, #88, #90, #93)
/admin Decap CMS coverage gaps filled — all editable content blocks on the live site now have corresponding CMS controls so the clinic can self-serve copy updates without code changes (PR #91)

gaps.title: No ACC landing page
gaps.why: Neck-pain and back-pain condition pages now ship (items #22, #23 closed). The remaining piece of G7 is a single indexable /acc/ URL — osteo.co.nz still owns 'osteopath acc christchurch' and we have ACC mentions scattered across the site but no canonical landing. Reinforced 2026-05-29: thehouseofacupuncture.co.nz/our-services/acc-acupuncture-chistchurch still owns 'acupuncture acc christchurch' for the same structural reason. Scoped under item #25.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months. April-2026 industry data: 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within 30 days, 50% of Perplexity citations come from content less than 13 weeks old. osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine/cranial/baby-osteo queries on blog-post velocity — 1 short post per condition is enough to claim those niches. MK Osteopathy is doing the same for TMJ. May-2026 BrightEdge / Healthcare Success data confirms the pattern is hardening: AI Overviews now trigger on 88% of healthcare queries (up from 72% in 2024), with cited brands earning 35% more clicks and AI visitors converting 4.4× higher than organic. Reinforced 2026-05-29 by Ahrefs week-of-26-May data: only 38% of AI Overview citations now come from top-10 organic results (down from 76% in Jul-2025) on the back of Gemini 3's query fan-out — pages with topical depth rank-anywhere can still be cited, so blog freshness directly compounds with the existing condition pages even before they reach top-10.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit appears in ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations (May 2026 CMSWire/Conductor data). Reddit is the second most visible website in Google search results in 2026 (only Wikipedia ahead) and is now the most-cited domain across all major AI models. Tinuiti's Q1 2026 AI Citations Trends Report shows social-media share of AI citations climbed past 9% Oct-2025 → Jan-2026, with Reddit accounting for the dominant share of that growth across nine product categories. ClaudeBot crawl volume grew 800% at the start of 2026 as Anthropic scaled its web search API — Reddit citations are increasingly the path into Claude's answers too, not just Perplexity's. Item #15 still applies.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live. With the citation-ready 40–60 word answers now in place (item #31 shipped 2026-05-17), wrapping them in a speakable block is the natural follow-up — gives voice assistants a single span to read aloud rather than guessing.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache. Reinforced this week: post-March-2026 Google update weights review recency more heavily than raw count; GBP is the primary data source for AI-driven local search results per May-2026 PinMeTo / MedRankSEO data. May-2026 mapranks / icreatebrand confirm: Google is replacing Maps Q&A with Gemini-generated answers pulled from GBP + reviews + the clinic website, so review freshness now directly feeds the Maps-surface answer generation as well.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Ahrefs analysis of 17M AI citations (April 2026) found AI-cited content is 25.7% fresher than traditional Google organic results — 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within the last 30 days. Freshness compounds. Confirmed again 2026-05-29: osteopath-christchurch.com still owns headaches/cranial/baby/teeth-grinding/stress/arthritis queries on the strength of one short blog post per condition (most recent post dated 2026-04-02); MK Osteopathy still owns the TMJ slot the same way. Per BrightEdge May-2026 data, AI Overviews now trigger on 88% of healthcare queries (up from 72% in 2024). NEW DATA POINT 2026-05-29 (Ahrefs, week-of-26-May): only 38% of AI Overview citations now come from top-10 organic results, down from 76% in Jul-2025 on the back of Gemini 3's query fan-out — pages with topical depth rank-anywhere can still get cited, so blog freshness compounds even before the condition pages reach top-10. With /admin CMS coverage now filled (PR #91), the editorial path is shorter than it was — adding a Decap blog collection would let the clinic publish without code changes.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. Reddit is now ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations across major AI platforms (May 2026 Conductor / CMSWire data) and the second most visible site in Google results overall (Wikipedia first). Tinuiti Q1 2026 data: social-media share of AI citations climbed past 9% Oct-2025 → Jan-2026 with Reddit accounting for the dominant share of that growth. ClaudeBot scaled 800% at start of 2026 — Reddit is increasingly the path into Claude's answers too. 90/10 rule: 90% non-promotional, 2–4 weeks of karma-building before any mention of Meridian.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old. Reinforced 2026-05-02: Google's March 2026 core update now weights review recency AND owner response rate more heavily than raw count. Healthcare best-practice = at least 1 new review/week. May-2026 PinMeTo data: GBP is the primary data source for AI-driven local search across Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT and Perplexity location queries. Reinforced 2026-05-29 (mapranks / icreatebrand 2026-05-25): Google is replacing Maps Q&A with Gemini-generated answers pulled from GBP + reviews + the clinic website, so review freshness now directly feeds the Maps-surface answer generation too.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query (confirmed again 2026-05-29). We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Same gap reinforces 'acupuncture acc christchurch' (thehouseofacupuncture.co.nz/our-services/acc-acupuncture-chistchurch owns that one for the same structural reason). Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, who lodges the claim (Nina, our osteopath; Min for acupuncture-specific claims), how to lodge, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.

items.title: Add /conditions/concussion/ page — Triskelion Concussion Care owns 'concussion osteopath christchurch'
items.body: First check 2026-05-22 of 'concussion osteopath christchurch' showed triskelionconcussioncare.co.nz at #1, osteo.co.nz/concussion/ at #2, triskelionosteopathy.co.nz/concussion/ at #3; HeadWise (headwise.co.nz/who-we-are/andy-gibson) and Better Health (anastasia-mcpherson-osteopath-christchurch) also rank in the top 10, and Russell Johns at Moorhouse Osteopathic Centre is named explicitly in the AI summary. Reinforced 2026-05-29: same pattern — and the newly tracked 'post concussion osteopath christchurch' variant got its first check this run and shows the same Triskelion + Better Health + Halswell + Moorhouse set. Concussion is high-value, ACC-adjacent injury content (post-concussion symptoms — headaches, dizziness, fatigue, brain fog — overlap directly with the headache/migraine and neck pain conditions we already treat) and a single-condition specialist site (Triskelion Concussion Care) is currently the strongest competitor. The smallest fix is a /conditions/concussion/ page that mirrors the existing condition-page pattern (sciatica, neck-pain, back-pain, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery): MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, 40–60 word citation-ready intro (per #31's pattern), acute-vs-chronic / when-to-seek-urgent-care / ACC eligibility / how osteopathy fits / book-now sections. Add a corresponding entry to conditions.json and a tile in the homepage 'Common conditions we treat' grid. Page exists pattern to copy; effort S.

items.title: Add Claude-SearchBot + Claude-Web allow lines to robots.txt
items.body: Cloudflare's late-May 2026 crawler telemetry now lists Claude-SearchBot as a distinct user-agent at ~2% share of total AI-bot traffic, separate from the training-only ClaudeBot we already allow. Anthropic now operates three bot identities: ClaudeBot (training corpus crawl), anthropic-ai / Claude-Web (Claude.ai user-driven retrieval — we already allow anthropic-ai), and Claude-SearchBot (the new live-retrieval agent backing Claude's web search). Our robots.txt currently doesn't mention Claude-SearchBot or Claude-Web by name. Today this is fine because everything falls under the User-agent: * Allow: / default — but the moment we add ANY Disallow line (which is likely as the site grows /admin/, /book/<slug>/ targeting, etc.), Claude-SearchBot would inherit that restriction silently. Add explicit Allow lines for Claude-SearchBot and Claude-Web alongside the existing ClaudeBot / anthropic-ai entries to safeguard Claude.ai citation traffic against any future Disallow. Two-line change.

items.title: Add structured 5–7 item list block to every condition page
items.body: Search Engine Land's May 2026 analysis of 25,000 AI-cited URLs found list-formatted pages (ordered/unordered HTML lists inside H2/H3 sections, ~18 words per item) get cited at materially higher rates than equivalent prose paragraphs. Our citation-ready 40–60 word intros shipped via PR #77 (item #31) — adding a structured `<ul>` block per condition is the natural next-cheapest citation hook. Suggested structure per page: 'Common signs' (5-7 bullet symptoms), 'When to see an osteopath' (5-7 bullet triggers), 'What to expect at your first appointment' (5-7 bullet steps). Pair each list under its own H2 so the schema and the rendered HTML both surface the structure to AI crawlers. Six condition pages × ~30 minutes per page; no schema changes needed beyond the existing MedicalCondition + FAQPage. Effort S.
